name: OpenText Content Services & Information Management API
what_it_does: OpenText REST APIs for enterprise content management (ECM), information management, and business network platforms. Enables AI agents to manage document storage, version control, and metadata management via Content Server and Extended ECM for enterprise content governance, handle document capture and classification via Captiva intelligent capture for AP and HR automation, access records management and compliance hold data for information governance and regulatory compliance, retrieve business network and supply chain document exchange data via OpenText Trading Grid, handle customer communication management (CCM) data for personalized document generation, access contract and agreement management data for procurement and legal operations, retrieve digital asset management (DAM) data for marketing content operations, manage eDiscovery and legal hold data for litigation support, handle archiving and information lifecycle management for long-term retention compliance, and integrate content management data with SAP, Oracle, Salesforce, and Microsoft platforms.
use_cases: Automating document capture from AP and HR invoice processing agents
Managing records retention from information governance agents
Retrieving contract data from procurement compliance agents
Processing supply chain documents from EDI trading network agents
not_for: Simple cloud storage without enterprise ECM governance requirements
SMB document management without enterprise compliance and records management
Consumer file sharing without enterprise content management complexity
best_when: A large enterprise, government agency, or regulated industry organization using OpenText wants AI agents to automate document capture, records management, content governance, supply chain document exchange, and information lifecycle management.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: OpenText manages regulatory records and legal hold data — automated records deletion without records retention schedule review violates legal hold obligations and regulatory recordkeeping requirements. eDiscovery legal hold automation requires legal counsel authorization. Records declassification automation without records manager review creates regulatory compliance exposure. AP invoice capture automation must validate PO matching before payment authorization.

auth.notes: OpenText uses OAuth 2.0 (OIDC via OpenText Identity Service). Developer portal at developer.opentext.com. Waterloo, Ontario HQ. Public (OTEX NASDAQ). $5.7B+ annual revenue. Massive enterprise software company — acquired Documentum, Hummingbird, HP Software (content assets), and Micro Focus (2023, $5.8B). OpenText Content Cloud is the unified platform. Strong in government, financial services, and manufacturing ECM. Competes with Hyland, Laserfiche, and Microsoft SharePoint for ECM market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per product module, user count, and volume
pricing.notes: Waterloo, Ontario. OpenText Corporation (OTEX NASDAQ). Founded 1991. $5.7B+ revenue (2023). Major enterprise software company via acquisitions: Documentum (from EMC/Dell 2017), Hummingbird, Recommind, Actuate, HP Software assets, Micro Focus (2023). OpenText Content Cloud covers ECM, records, capture, CCM, DAM, and analytics. Competes with Hyland and Microsoft for ECM market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; product and deployment dependent

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: Records deletion and disposition automation must comply with records retention schedules and legal hold; automated disposition without records manager review violates legal and regulatory requirements
OpenText acquisition sprawl — OpenText has acquired 30+ companies; product portfolio includes Documentum, Content Server, Captiva, Exstream, and Micro Focus assets, each with separate API surfaces
Content Server vs. Content Cloud — OpenText has legacy Content Server (on-premise) and OpenText Cloud (SaaS) products; API endpoints and authentication differ between deployment models
SAP Extended ECM dependency — OpenText Extended ECM for SAP requires SAP integration framework; ECM data model tied to SAP business objects (purchase orders, contracts)
Micro Focus integration — OpenText acquired Micro Focus 2023 ($5.8B); product integration ongoing; Micro Focus products (ALM, Fortify) have separate APIs outside ECM portfolio
eDiscovery legal hold — legal hold via OpenText requires legal counsel authorization; automated hold placement and release must follow documented legal process
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise content management. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2/OIDC. US/EU/AU/CA. Enterprise documents, records, and regulated content.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise ECM. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Enterprise content and records data.
